sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
You begin with a sharp left-right-left chicane. Brake at about the 100m board, down to 3rd gear, and aim to cut as much of the turn 1 apex as you can. Immediately switch direction into turn 2 and hug the right curb — the rear can easily snap if youre too aggressive on the throttle. Turn 3 follows quickly, so keep the wheel steady and feed in power only when youre sure of traction.

These flowing corners are either flat or nearly flat, depending on downforce and tire wear. You should be in 6th or 7th gear, and the challenge is threading between walls while making slight steering adjustments. Stay planted and keep the car smooth; overcorrection here can mean race over. The circuit is narrow and blind — use reference points, such as trackside boards, to time your inputs.

Brake lightly and steer with precision. Taken in 5th gear, its a rhythm-based section. You do not want to scrub speed here; small throttle lifts are better than braking. Set yourself up cleanly for the run into Sector 2.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
This is a fast left-right-left sequence where you will want to carry as much speed as possible. Use 4th or 5th gear, flick the car directionally while balancing the throttle. Try to stay off the harsh inside kerbs, they can bounce you into the wall. This section rewards commitment and car placement. Do not lift too much or you will lose heaps of time.

Another high-speed, flowing sequence. Minimal braking is needed — just a lift or small tap at turn 14, staying in 6th gear. Then prepare for turn 16, which is slightly tighter and requires more steering input. Use all the track, but dont kiss the wall on exit; it comes up fast.

This section is arguably the trickiest of the track. Turns 17 to 20 are taken almost flat, but youre constantly adjusting the cars balance. Stay in 6th–7th gear, but modulate the throttle where necessary. Its easy to miss the racing line due to poor visibility — keep calm and smooth. Oversteer is common here, so adjust your differential settings once you have a few laps of experience under your belt, or lift earlier and adjust your line if needed.

Another flat-out set of kinks leading into the DRS zone. Stay planted and precise, let the car drift right as you line up for the long, flat-out blast to the final sector.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
Open DRS down the back straight and prepare for turn 24, a flat-out left-hand bend taken in 7th gear. Hug the wall closely, but dont touch it! Minor scrapes here can escalate into game-ending crashes. Trust the car and your inputs.

Brake hard at around 100m for turn 27 — the last and only real heavy braking zone of the lap. Drop to 2nd gear and clip the inside curb without riding it too much. Get a strong exit here — it leads onto the long pit straight with DRS. Any time lost here costs you all the way to turn 1.

Open the DRS early. This is one of the best overtaking zones on the track, so get the power down cleanly and defend or attack depending on your racing situation!
